candidates.why: Primary top-line revenue label; values match Total Revenue and should be used as first priority per rule to avoid duplicated totals.
Duplicate total of Revenue; kept as secondary priority in case labeling changes over time.
Finance income, not operating revenue; should not be used for REVT.
Profit subtotal, not revenue.
Result from discontinued operations, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Income Available to Com Excl ExtraOrd
candidates.why: Earnings measure, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Income Available to Com Incl ExtraOrd
candidates.why: Earnings measure, not revenue.
Liability, not revenue flow.
Equity/OCI component, not revenue.

variables.notes: Revenue and Total Revenue are identical numerically; Revenue is selected as primary per priority rule, with Total Revenue as a backup label.

candidates.why: Best available proxy for total operating costs including cost of sales, given lack of a separate Cost of goods sold line.
Represents SG&A, a component of operating expenses rather than direct COGS; not ideal as standalone COGS.
Appears to be a subtotal/alternative measure; less standard than Operating expenses for COGS proxy.
Non-operating; should not be used as COGS.
Specific adjustment item, not COGS.
R&D, not direct cost of sales.
Component of operating overhead, not full COGS.
Balance sheet inventory component, not an income statement COGS line.
Balance sheet inventory component, not an income statement COGS line.

candidates.row_label: Other accrued income and prepaid expenses
candidates.why: Balance sheet accruals, not COGS.
Liability, not COGS line.

variables.notes: No explicit Cost of goods sold line exists; total Operating expenses is the closest available proxy for direct and indirect operating costs, so it is used for COGS.

candidates.why: High-level total of operating costs that likely includes COGS and various overheads; per rules, totals should be avoided when component rows exist for SG&A mapping.
Classic SG&A-type overhead line; directly represents selling/general/administrative expenses.
Other operating gains/losses; can be treated as an SG&A-type overhead component rather than COGS.

variables.notes: Administrative expenses is a clear SG&A component. Net Other Operating Loss is treated as an additional overhead component. Operating expenses is a broad total that likely includes COGS, so it is excluded per the rule to avoid totals when components are available.

candidates.why: Explicit R&D expense line item on the income statement.

variables.notes: A dedicated Research and Development Expenses row exists and is used directly for XRD.

candidates.why: Represents allocation of net income to non-controlling shareholders, not interest expense.
Duplicate concept of non-controlling interests in the income statement, not interest expense.
Equity component, not interest expense.
Supplemental lease-related disclosure on the balance sheet, not the primary income statement interest expense line.
Interest income rather than expense; sign is opposite of what is needed for XINT.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. (only number until 2006) The true interest expense is reported as 'Finance costs' on the income statement, which is not included in the provided candidate list. That label is selected as the primary XINT proxy because it clearly represents net finance/interest costs. Manual review is flagged since the choice comes from outside the candidate list and to confirm that Finance costs indeed captures all interest expense for this issuer. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Finance costs

candidates.why: Income from equity-method investments, not book equity.

candidates.row_label: Equity attributable to shareholders of the parent company
candidates.why: Represents book equity attributable to owners/parent, which is the preferred base for BE excluding non-controlling interests.
Includes non-controlling interests; not preferred when parent equity is available separately.
Technical balancing row, not the primary equity measure.
Represents total assets/equity+liabilities, not equity alone.

final_choice.row_label: Equity attributable to shareholders of the parent company

variables.notes: Parent-company equity is explicitly provided and excludes non-controlling interests, matching the BE definition and avoiding double-counting with MIB.

candidates.why: Represents the income statement share of net income to non-controlling interests; a flow, not the balance sheet amount, but confirms the presence of NCI.
Duplicate income statement label for non-controlling interests; again a flow, not the stock.
Balance sheet line for the equity of minority shareholders; this is the correct stock measure for MIB.

variables.notes: Balance sheet Non-controlling interests provides the correct minority equity amount to pair with BE, avoiding income-statement flow measures.
